masthead.dek: Every question is a best-without: the biggest, best, or most-decorated that famously never won the big one — across sports, geography, and culture. Reason to the one that fits the slicer. Narrow it down, place your bet, then tap to check.
masthead.footer_html: Built fresh against the existing best-without set. Verified to official award bodies, league sources, Britannica, and WorldAtlas.

dimensions: most major golf runner-ups, no major win
most F1 race wins, no title
most NHL goals, no Stanley Cup
highest FIFA ranking, no World Cup appearance
men's world No. 1, no Grand Slam title
most Olympic medals (individual), no gold
most NBA career points, no title
most NFL passing yards, no Super Bowl win
most NFL coaching wins, no title
best men's cricket team, no World Cup title
longest active NBA title drought
longest active Stanley Cup drought
most European Cup finals lost
only Six Nations nation, no title
biggest soccer nation, no Euro title
100m world-record holder, no individual Olympic medal
oldest NFL franchise, no Super Bowl appearance
women's world No. 1, no Grand Slam title
most MLB home runs, no World Series
most NBA points, no All-Star selection
second-largest landlocked country by area
highest peak that ranks only second on its continent
longest river with no outlet to the ocean
largest desert overall (cold rather than hot)
longest river contained within one country
largest country wholly south of the equator
most populous landlocked country
most populous island
largest country bordering exactly one neighbor
largest continuous body of sand
longest lake
largest country in Africa by area
tallest mountain measured base to summit
largest South American country other than Brazil
highest volcano (outranked by a non-volcanic neighbor)
largest freshwater lake by surface area
deepest point of the Atlantic Ocean
largest country wholly within Europe
highest peak in the contiguous United States
largest country without a standing army
most Oscar nominations of any kind, no competitive win
most acting Oscar nominations, no win
Best Director nominations, no win (a major name)
most Primetime Emmy acting nominations, no win
most Grammy nominations by a performer, no win
most Hot 100 Top 40 hits, no No. 1
most weeks at No. 2, never No. 1 (single track)
most CMA Award nominations, no win
most Oscar nominations by a film, zero wins
most PGA Tour wins, no major
most Tony acting nominations, no win
most Nobel Prize nominations, no win
most Best Director nominations among never-winners
most Eurovision second-place finishes
most consecutive Daytime Emmy losses before a first win
most Top 40 albums, no Hot 100 No. 1 single
honorary Oscar, only one competitive nod and no win (high-grossing star)
plan.note: All best-without / perennial-runner-up slicers across sports, geography/nature, and culture/awards. Answers route to a person, team, place, or count, fresh dimensions throughout.

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · most major golf
questions.dimension: most major golf runner-ups, no major win
questions.q: Among golfers who never won one of the four men's major championships, which player holds the record for finishing as runner-up in a major the most times?
questions.detail: The Scot finished second in five majors without ever winning one: the U.S. Open in 1994, 1997, and 2006, the PGA Championship in 1995, and the Open Championship in 2005.
questions.why: A sharp player narrows the field of great major-less golfers to Montgomerie, Sergio Garcia, Lee Westwood, and a few old-timers. Garcia and a handful of others top out at four seconds, and Garcia later won the 2017 Masters anyway, so Montgomerie's five second-place finishes stand alone.
questions.more: Five golfers are tied for second on this list at four runner-up finishes each, including Macdonald Smith, Harry Cooper, Doug Sanders, Bruce Crampton, and Garcia (before his Masters win).
questions.source: ESPN 'Ask Steven' (espn.com/golf) and Golf Monthly, most runner-up finishes in men's majors

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · most F1 race wi
questions.dimension: most F1 race wins, no title
questions.q: Which driver won the most Formula One Grand Prix races in his career without ever capturing the World Drivers' Championship?
questions.detail: Moss won 16 Grands Prix from 66 starts and finished runner-up in the championship four straight years (1955-1958), losing the 1958 title to Mike Hawthorn by a single point.
questions.why: The famous near-misses are Moss and modern drivers like Valtteri Bottas. Bottas tops out around 10 wins, so Moss's 16 victories make him the clear all-time leader among the title-less.
questions.more: Moss is often called the greatest driver never to win the F1 championship; he also finished third in the standings three times.
questions.source: ESPN F1 and bet365 News, most F1 wins without a world championship

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · most NHL goals,
questions.dimension: most NHL goals, no Stanley Cup
questions.q: Which player scored the most goals in his NHL career but never won the Stanley Cup?
questions.detail: Dionne scored 731 goals, fifth-most when he retired, across stints with the Red Wings, Kings, and Rangers, yet he never reached a Stanley Cup Final.
questions.why: Among great ring-less scorers the candidates are Dionne, Mike Gartner, Mats Sundin, and Jarome Iginla. Dionne's 731 goals outrank them all without a Cup, making him the record holder.
questions.more: Of the eight players with 700-plus NHL goals, only Dionne and Gartner never won a championship.
questions.source: NHL.com 100 Greatest Players and The Hockey Writers, NHL.com career goal totals

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · highest FIFA ra
questions.dimension: highest FIFA ranking, no World Cup appearance
questions.q: Which country's men's national soccer team reached the highest spot in the FIFA World Rankings while never qualifying for a World Cup?
questions.detail: Finland climbed to 33rd in the FIFA Men's World Ranking in 2007 yet had never qualified for a World Cup finals tournament.
questions.why: The pool of never-qualified nations is small once you exclude tiny states. Finland's peak ranking of 33rd is higher than any other country that has never reached a World Cup.
questions.more: Finland did break through to its first major tournament at UEFA Euro 2020, but a World Cup appearance still eluded the team.
questions.source: Wikipedia FIFA Men's World Ranking and WorldAtlas, teams never to qualify for the World Cup

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · men's world No.
questions.dimension: men's world No. 1, no Grand Slam title
questions.q: Which men's tennis player reached the world No. 1 ranking yet retired without ever winning a Grand Slam singles title?
questions.detail: The Chilean spent six weeks atop the ATP rankings in 1998 but lost his only major final, the 1998 Australian Open, to Petr Korda.
questions.why: Many fine players never won a slam, but reaching No. 1 without one is a tiny club. Among the men, Rios is the only one to have held the top ranking without a major, which is what makes him the answer.
questions.more: Rios won five Masters Series titles, at the time a record for a player with no Grand Slam.
questions.source: Tennis365 and Perfect Tennis, players who reached No. 1 without a Grand Slam

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · most Olympic me
questions.dimension: most Olympic medals (individual), no gold
questions.q: Among individual athletes, who won the most Olympic medals across a career without ever winning a gold?
questions.detail: The German swimmer collected 10 Olympic medals from 1992 to 2004, four silver and six bronze in freestyle and relay events, but never a gold.
questions.why: Reasoning points toward perennial-podium athletes in swimming or track. Van Almsick's 10 medals are the most by any individual who never struck gold, edging out other multi-medal silver-and-bronze careers.
questions.more: She won her first Olympic medals at Barcelona 1992 at age 14.
questions.source: Olympedia and Swimming World Magazine athlete profile

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · most NBA career
questions.dimension: most NBA career points, no title
questions.q: Which player accumulated the most points in NBA history without ever winning a championship?
questions.detail: Malone scored 36,928 points, second on the all-time list, over 19 seasons but never won a ring, losing two Finals with Utah and one with the Lakers.
questions.why: The great ring-less scorers are Malone, Carmelo Anthony, and Chris Paul. Malone's nearly 37,000 points dwarf the others, so the slicer lands on him.
questions.more: Malone also holds the record for the most career NBA playoff games without a title.
questions.source: ESPN, NBA players with the most points and no championships

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · most NFL passin
questions.dimension: most NFL passing yards, no Super Bowl win
questions.q: Which quarterback threw for the most career passing yards in NFL history without ever winning a Super Bowl?
questions.detail: Marino passed for 61,361 yards and 420 touchdowns in 17 seasons with Miami; his only Super Bowl trip ended in a loss to the 49ers after the 1984 season.
questions.why: The ring-less passing greats include Marino, Fran Tarkenton, and Dan Fouts. Marino's total tops them all, making him the leader among quarterbacks who never lifted the trophy.
questions.more: Marino set single-season records for yards and touchdowns in 1984 and entered the Hall of Fame in 2005.
questions.source: Pro Football Hall of Fame and Pro-Football-Reference, Dan Marino career totals

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · most NFL coachi
questions.dimension: most NFL coaching wins, no title
questions.q: Which head coach won the most regular-season and playoff games in NFL history without ever winning a Super Bowl or league championship?
questions.detail: Schottenheimer compiled a 200-126-1 regular-season record with four teams and finished with 205 total wins, eighth-most all-time, but never won a championship.
questions.why: Once Andy Reid won a Super Bowl, the winningest coach without a title became Schottenheimer. He is the only coach with 200-plus wins and no championship, which pins the answer.
questions.more: His teams reached the playoffs 13 times but often lost in painful fashion, a pattern dubbed 'Martyball.'
questions.source: CBS Sports and ABC News obituary, Schottenheimer career win total

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · best men's cric
questions.dimension: best men's cricket team, no World Cup title
questions.q: Which men's national cricket team is widely considered the best never to have won a World Cup, despite repeated deep runs in the sport's global tournaments?
questions.detail: South Africa reached the 2024 T20 World Cup final and four 50-over World Cup semifinals (1992, 1999, 2007, 2015) but has never won a senior ICC World Cup.
questions.why: Strong test-and-ODI sides that never won the trophy point to South Africa over the Netherlands or others. Their long history of semifinal exits, the 'chokers' reputation, makes them the standout answer.
questions.more: South Africa lost the 2024 men's T20 final to India by seven runs, their closest call yet.
questions.source: Wikipedia 2024 Men's T20 World Cup final and Topendsports, South Africa World Cup history

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · longest active
questions.dimension: longest active NBA title drought
questions.q: Which NBA franchise has the longest active championship drought, having not won a title in roughly three-quarters of a century?
questions.detail: The franchise last won the NBA championship in 1951 as the Rochester Royals, a drought of about 75 years, the longest active in the league.
questions.why: The clue 'has won, but long ago' rules out never-champions like the Suns and points to historic franchises. The Kings' 1951 title makes their drought the longest ongoing, not the Suns who never won at all.
questions.more: The franchise moved from Rochester to Cincinnati, then Kansas City, then Sacramento in 1985 without reaching another Finals.
questions.source: Yahoo Sports and TheBigLead, longest NBA championship droughts

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · longest active
questions.dimension: longest active Stanley Cup drought
questions.q: Which NHL team has the longest active Stanley Cup drought, despite a long history of past championships?
questions.detail: Toronto last won the Stanley Cup in 1967 and has not even reached a Final since, a drought of around 59 seasons, the longest active in the NHL.
questions.why: The slicer is 'former champion, longest current gap,' which excludes never-winners. Toronto's 1967 title and decades without a Final make them the answer over teams that never won.
questions.more: The Leafs own 13 Stanley Cups but none in the post-expansion era.
questions.source: The Hockey Writers and Wikipedia, list of NHL franchise post-season droughts

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · most European C
questions.dimension: most European Cup finals lost
questions.q: Which big-name European soccer club has lost the most finals of Europe's top club competition, the European Cup and Champions League?
questions.detail: Juventus has reached nine finals of the European Cup/Champions League but lost seven of them, the most defeats of any club, winning only in 1985 and 1996.
questions.why: Repeat final losers include Juventus, Benfica, and Bayern's old sides. Juventus's seven final defeats are the record, so the best-without-the-prize-this-often angle lands on them.
questions.more: Their final losses span four decades, from 1973 to 2017.
questions.source: Kickest and footboom, Juventus Champions League final record

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · only Six Nation
questions.dimension: only Six Nations nation, no title
questions.q: Which national team is the only one in rugby's Six Nations Championship that has never won the tournament title?
questions.detail: Italy joined the championship in 2000 and through 2026 has never finished first, with a best result of fourth in 2013, while also collecting the most wooden spoons for last place.
questions.why: Five of the six nations have multiple titles; only one never has. Reasoning eliminates the traditional powers and the occasional winners, leaving Italy as the lone title-less member.
questions.more: Italy has finished bottom of the table in 18 of its first 26 campaigns.
questions.source: Rugby World Six Nations winners list and bet365, Six Nations wooden spoons

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · biggest soccer
questions.dimension: biggest soccer nation, no Euro title
questions.q: Among nations that have played in the men's UEFA European Championship, which is the biggest soccer power never to win the tournament?
questions.detail: England has appeared at 10 European Championship finals and reached the final in 2020 and 2024, losing both, but has never won the trophy.
questions.why: Several large nations have won the Euros, so the slicer cuts to the biggest one that hasn't. England, despite two recent finals, is repeatedly named the largest never to lift the cup.
questions.more: England lost the Euro 2024 final to Spain on a late Mikel Oyarzabal goal.
questions.source: GiveMeSport and NBC Sports, biggest teams never to win the Euros

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · 100m world-reco
questions.dimension: 100m world-record holder, no individual Olympic medal
questions.q: Which sprinter once held the 100 meters world record yet never won an individual medal at the Olympic Games?
questions.detail: The Jamaican broke the 100m world record twice (9.77 and 9.74) but finished fifth in the Olympic 100m final in both 2004 and 2008, never reaching an individual podium.
questions.why: World-record sprinters who flopped at the Olympics narrow to Powell. His only Olympic golds came in relays, leaving him as the famed record-holder with no individual medal.
questions.more: His 2008 relay gold was later stripped after a teammate's doping case, though he kept a 2016 relay gold.
questions.source: Olympics.com and Olympics Wiki athlete profile, Asafa Powell

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · oldest NFL fran
questions.dimension: oldest NFL franchise, no Super Bowl appearance
questions.q: Which is the longest-established NFL franchise that has never even reached a Super Bowl?
questions.detail: Founded in 1930, the Lions are the oldest of the four current teams that have never appeared in a Super Bowl and remain the only NFC franchise never to reach one.
questions.why: The clue is 'never even got there,' not 'never won,' which excludes the Vikings and other repeat losers. Among the four never-appearers, the Lions are the oldest, making them the answer.
questions.more: The other never-appearing teams are the Browns and the expansion-era Jaguars and Texans.
questions.source: Yahoo Sports and ESPN, NFL teams that have never reached the Super Bowl

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · women's world N
questions.dimension: women's world No. 1, no Grand Slam title
questions.q: Which woman reached the world No. 1 ranking in tennis but never won a Grand Slam singles title?
questions.detail: Safina topped the WTA rankings in 2009 and reached three major finals (two French Opens and one Australian Open) but lost all three and never won a slam.
questions.why: Holding No. 1 without a major is rare on the women's tour too, with Safina and Jelena Jankovic the notable cases. Safina's three lost finals make her the headline example of a No. 1 with no title.
questions.more: Her brother Marat Safin, by contrast, won two Grand Slam titles.
questions.source: Tennis365 and Tennis Majors, best WTA players never to win a Grand Slam

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · most MLB home r
questions.dimension: most MLB home runs, no World Series
questions.q: Which slugger hit the most home runs in Major League Baseball history without ever winning a World Series?
questions.detail: Bonds hit a record 762 career home runs but never won a World Series; his closest chance came in 2002, when the Giants lost to the Angels.
questions.why: Ring-less power hitters include Bonds, Ken Griffey Jr., and Ernie Banks. Bonds's all-time-leading 762 homers put him clearly atop the list of champions-that-never-were.
questions.more: Bonds also won a record seven MVP awards without a title.
questions.source: Wikipedia MLB career home run leaders and GamerKoala, players who never won a World Series

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · most NBA points
questions.dimension: most NBA points, no All-Star selection
questions.q: Which player scored the most career points in the NBA without ever being selected to an All-Star Game?
questions.detail: Crawford scored 19,419 points over a 20-year career and won three Sixth Man of the Year awards but was never named an All-Star.
questions.why: The non-All-Star scoring list points to bench scorers like Crawford and Eddie Johnson. Crawford passed Johnson in 2019 to claim the most career points by a player never named an All-Star.
questions.more: He is the only three-time Sixth Man of the Year in NBA history.
questions.source: StatMuse and Interbasket, most NBA career points without an All-Star appearance

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · second-largest
questions.dimension: second-largest landlocked country by area
questions.q: Forget the giant that tops the list. What is the second-largest country in the world with no coastline at all, a vast, thinly peopled land squeezed between two huge neighbors?
questions.detail: At about 1,564,000 sq km, Mongolia is the second-largest landlocked country on Earth, trailing only Kazakhstan, and is wedged between Russia and China.
questions.why: A sharp player lists big interior countries and quickly removes Kazakhstan (the obvious No. 1). The runner-up is the catch: it is Mongolia, not a more populous candidate.
questions.more: Mongolia is also the world's most sparsely populated sovereign country, with roughly two people per square kilometer.
questions.source: WorldAtlas, 'Largest Landlocked Countries In The World'; MDPI study, 'Land Use Hotspots of the Two Largest Landlocked Countries: Kazakhstan and Mongolia'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · highest peak th
questions.dimension: highest peak that ranks only second on its continent
questions.q: Many peaks are the tallest on their continent. Which mountain is only the SECOND-highest on its own continent, yet still towers as the second-highest on the entire planet?
questions.detail: K2 (8,611 m), in the Karakoram on the China-Pakistan border, is second to Everest in Asia and worldwide; every other continent's runner-up peak is far lower.
questions.why: The set is the seven 'second summits.' All but one are modest by global standards, so the world's No. 2 peak being a mere continental runner-up is the twist.
questions.more: K2 is widely considered the hardest of the fourteen 8,000-meter peaks to climb.
questions.source: Britannica, 'K2'; Wikipedia, 'Seven Second Summits'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · longest river w
questions.dimension: longest river with no outlet to the ocean
questions.q: The world's most famous rivers all spill into a sea or ocean. What is the longest river on Earth whose water never reaches an ocean, draining instead into a giant inland sea?
questions.detail: The Volga runs about 3,530 km across western Russia and empties into the landlocked Caspian Sea, making it the longest endorheic (no-ocean-outlet) river in the world.
questions.why: Players hunt for famous long rivers but must drop every one that reaches the sea. The Volga survives because it dies into the Caspian, and it is also Europe's longest river.
questions.more: The Volga's basin covers about two-fifths of European Russia and holds nearly half that region's people.
questions.source: Britannica, 'Volga River'; NASA Earth Observatory, 'The Volga River flows into the Caspian Sea'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · largest desert
questions.dimension: largest desert overall (cold rather than hot)
questions.q: Everyone names the Sahara as the planet's biggest desert. But deserts are defined by dryness, not heat. What is actually the largest desert on Earth?
questions.detail: At about 14.2 million sq km, the Antarctic polar desert dwarfs the Sahara's roughly 9.2 million sq km; its interior gets less than 50 mm of precipitation a year.
questions.why: A reasoner who recalls that 'desert' means low precipitation, not sand or heat, can leap past the Sahara to the frozen continent. The Sahara is only the largest HOT desert.
questions.more: Antarctica is also the coldest and windiest place on Earth.
questions.source: Britannica/NASA via Discover Wildlife, 'The world's largest desert'; National Geographic Education, 'Deserts'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · longest river c
questions.dimension: longest river contained within one country
questions.q: Plenty of long rivers cross several nations. What is the longest river that flows from source to mouth entirely inside a single country?
questions.detail: The Yangtze runs about 6,300 km from the Tibetan Plateau to the East China Sea without ever leaving China, the longest river to stay within one nation.
questions.why: Players must screen out the Nile, Amazon, and Mississippi-Missouri (all longer but border-crossing or single-basin debate aside) and find the longest that touches just one country.
questions.more: It is the third-longest river in the world overall, after the Nile and the Amazon.
questions.source: Britannica, 'Yangtze River'; TravelChinaGuide, '10 Key Yangtze River Facts'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · largest country
questions.dimension: largest country wholly south of the equator
questions.q: The biggest country in the Southern Hemisphere actually pokes above the equator. So which is the largest country lying ENTIRELY south of the equator?
questions.detail: Australia (about 7.69 million sq km) sits completely below the equator; Brazil is bigger overall but straddles the line into the Northern Hemisphere.
questions.why: The trap is Brazil. Once a player remembers Brazil crosses the equator, the largest fully-southern country becomes Australia.
questions.more: Only Australia and Antarctica are continents lying entirely in the Southern Hemisphere.
questions.source: WorldAtlas, 'Countries Located Completely in the Southern Hemisphere'; WorldPopulationReview, 'Southern Hemisphere Countries'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · most populous l
questions.dimension: most populous landlocked country
questions.q: Many crowded countries sit on a coast. What is the most populous country in the world that has no sea coast at all?
questions.detail: With well over 100 million people, Ethiopia is the most populous fully landlocked country on Earth; it lost its coastline when Eritrea became independent in 1993.
questions.why: Players sift big populations and strike out every coastal giant. Ethiopia survives because it is large in people yet entirely interior.
questions.more: Ethiopia relies heavily on neighboring Djibouti's ports for sea trade.
questions.source: Britannica, 'Landlocked country'; WorldPopulationReview, 'Landlocked Countries'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · most populous i
questions.dimension: most populous island
questions.q: This single island holds more people than all but a handful of entire countries, yet it is not a nation of its own. Which island is the most populous on Earth?
questions.detail: Java is home to roughly 150 million-plus people, more than any other island, and is the heart of Indonesia rather than a sovereign state.
questions.why: Players think of big nations, but the prompt wants an island. Java packs more people than Russia or Japan, yet it is only part of a country.
questions.more: About one in fifty people on Earth lives on Java.
questions.source: Guinness World Records, 'Highest population, island'; Tempo, 'Top 10 Most Populous Islands in the World'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · largest country
questions.dimension: largest country bordering exactly one neighbor
questions.q: Most large countries touch several neighbors. Which is the largest country in the world that shares a land border with only one other country?
questions.detail: Canada, the world's second-largest country by area, touches only the United States by land, despite having the longest land border on Earth.
questions.why: Players test big countries and keep finding multiple neighbors. Canada is the surprise: enormous, but bordered by a single nation.
questions.more: Russia, by contrast, borders fourteen countries.
questions.source: WorldAtlas, 'Countries With Only One Neighbor'; Wikipedia, 'List of countries that border only one other country'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · largest continu
questions.dimension: largest continuous body of sand
questions.q: The Sahara is far bigger overall, but it is mostly rock and gravel, not dunes. Where on Earth is the largest unbroken expanse of sand?
questions.detail: The Rub' al-Khali, covering about 650,000 sq km mainly in Saudi Arabia, is the largest area of continuous sand in the world.
questions.why: Players default to the Sahara, but most of the Sahara isn't sand seas. The biggest single sand sheet is the Empty Quarter.
questions.more: It spills into Yemen, Oman, and the United Arab Emirates as well.
questions.source: Britannica, 'Rub al-Khali'; WorldAtlas, 'Rub al Khali, Empty Quarter Of The Arabian Desert'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · longest lake
questions.dimension: longest lake
questions.q: It is not the biggest lake, and it is not the deepest, yet from end to end no freshwater lake stretches farther. Which lake is the longest in the world?
questions.detail: Lake Tanganyika in East Africa runs about 660 km, the longest freshwater lake; it is also the second-deepest, behind Lake Baikal.
questions.why: Players reach for the biggest or deepest lakes, but 'longest' is a different axis. Tanganyika wins length while ceding both of the showier records.
questions.more: It borders Tanzania, the DRC, Burundi, and Zambia.
questions.source: Britannica, 'Lake Tanganyika'; Lakepedia, 'Lake Tanganyika'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · largest country
questions.dimension: largest country in Africa by area
questions.q: It is not Nigeria, not Egypt, not the old Sudan. Since 2011, which country has been the largest in Africa by land area?
questions.detail: At about 2.38 million sq km, Algeria became Africa's largest country when Sudan split in 2011, ahead of the Democratic Republic of the Congo.
questions.why: Many guess Sudan (out of date), or the more famous Egypt or Nigeria. Algeria is the quiet record-holder, much of it Sahara.
questions.more: Algeria is also the largest country bordering the Mediterranean Sea.
questions.source: Britannica, 'List of African countries by area'; WorldPopulationReview, 'Largest Countries in Africa'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · tallest mountai
questions.dimension: tallest mountain measured base to summit
questions.q: Measured from base to summit rather than from sea level, which mountain is actually the tallest on Earth, beating Everest by more than a kilometer?
questions.detail: Mauna Kea rises about 10,200 m from its deep ocean-floor base to its peak, taller than Everest's 8,849 m, though only about 4,200 m sits above sea level.
questions.why: Everest is the highest above sea level, but the prompt switches the measuring stick to base-to-top, which crowns a Hawaiian volcano.
questions.more: More than half of Mauna Kea is hidden beneath the Pacific.
questions.source: BBC Science Focus, 'What is the tallest mountain in the world?'; Moss and Fog, 'Mauna Kea Is the Tallest Mountain From Base to Summit'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · largest South A
questions.dimension: largest South American country other than Brazil
questions.q: Brazil dominates the continent, but which is the largest country in South America that is NOT Brazil, a land that also ranks eighth-largest in the whole world?
questions.detail: Argentina covers about 2.78 million sq km, the second-largest in South America after Brazil and the eighth-largest country on Earth.
questions.why: With Brazil removed by the question, players weigh Argentina against Peru, Colombia, and Bolivia. Argentina is the clear runner-up.
questions.more: Argentina stretches from subtropical jungle in the north to near-Antarctic Tierra del Fuego in the south.
questions.source: WorldAtlas, 'The Largest Countries In South America'; WorldPopulationReview, 'Largest Countries in South America'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · highest volcano
questions.dimension: highest volcano (outranked by a non-volcanic neighbor)
questions.q: It is the highest volcano on the planet, yet it is not even the highest mountain in its own corner of the world. Which volcano holds this odd double distinction?
questions.detail: Ojos del Salado (about 6,880 m), on the Chile-Argentina border, is the world's highest volcano but is topped by nearby Aconcagua, a non-volcanic peak, as the highest mountain in the Americas.
questions.why: A player recalls Aconcagua as the regional giant and might assume it is a volcano. The highest VOLCANO is the slightly lower Ojos del Salado.
questions.more: Its summit area holds a small crater lake said to be among the highest lakes on Earth.
questions.source: Smithsonian Global Volcanism Program, 'Nevados Ojos del Salado'; WorldAtlas, 'Ojos del Salado'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · largest freshwa
questions.dimension: largest freshwater lake by surface area
questions.q: The Caspian is far bigger, but it is salty. Which lake is the largest body of FRESH water in the world by surface area?
questions.detail: Lake Superior covers about 82,100 sq km, the largest freshwater lake on Earth by surface area; the bigger Caspian Sea is salt water.
questions.why: Players who reach for the Caspian as the 'largest lake' are filtered out by the word 'fresh.' The biggest drinkable lake is Superior.
questions.more: Superior holds about ten percent of the world's surface fresh water.
questions.source: Britannica, 'World's largest lakes'; WorldAtlas, 'The Largest Freshwater Lakes In The World'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · deepest point o
questions.dimension: deepest point of the Atlantic Ocean
questions.q: The Pacific has the famous deep trenches. What is the deepest point in any other ocean, the bottom of the Atlantic?
questions.detail: The Milwaukee Deep in the Puerto Rico Trench reaches about 8,380 m, the deepest point in the Atlantic Ocean.
questions.why: Everyone knows the Pacific's Mariana Trench. The prompt asks for the deepest spot in a different ocean, routing the answer to the Caribbean's edge.
questions.more: The trench runs roughly parallel to Puerto Rico's northern coast and is a known earthquake zone.
questions.source: Britannica, 'Puerto Rico Trench'; Britannica, 'Milwaukee Depth'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · largest country
questions.dimension: largest country wholly within Europe
questions.q: Russia is bigger, but it sprawls across two continents. Which is the largest country lying ENTIRELY within Europe?
questions.detail: Ukraine covers about 603,500 sq km, the largest country sitting completely inside Europe; Russia is larger but is transcontinental.
questions.why: The catch is Russia, which spans Europe and Asia. Once it is set aside, Ukraine edges out France as the largest fully-European country.
questions.more: Ukraine is about nine percent larger than France, the next-biggest wholly European country.
questions.source: Mappr, 'Largest Countries in Europe'; Homework.Study.com, 'Is Ukraine the biggest country in Europe?'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · highest peak in
questions.dimension: highest peak in the contiguous United States
questions.q: Alaska's Denali is the U.S. high point, but it sits off on its own. Which mountain is the highest in the lower 48 states?
questions.detail: Mount Whitney in California's Sierra Nevada rises to about 4,421 m (14,505 ft), the highest summit in the contiguous United States.
questions.why: Denali in detached Alaska is the obvious U.S. champion. The prompt limits the field to the connected lower 48, where Whitney tops out.
questions.more: Whitney stands only about 130 km from Badwater Basin in Death Valley, the lowest point in North America.
questions.source: Britannica, 'Mount Whitney'; WorldAtlas, 'Mount Whitney'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · largest country
questions.dimension: largest country without a standing army
questions.q: Some small nations skip having a military. What is the largest country in the world that has no standing army?
questions.detail: Costa Rica, about 51,100 sq km, is the largest country with no standing army, having abolished its military in 1948.
questions.why: Players think of microstates like Vatican City or Liechtenstein, but the prompt asks for the LARGEST. Costa Rica is far bigger than those and still army-free.
questions.more: It maintains only a civil police force for internal security.
questions.source: Army Technology, 'The world's biggest countries without armed forces'; Geography Worlds, 'Countries With No Military'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · most Oscar nomi
questions.dimension: most Oscar nominations of any kind, no competitive win
questions.q: One songwriter has been nominated for the Best Original Song Oscar more times than anyone has ever been nominated for any Oscar without winning. Who holds that all-time losing record?
questions.detail: As of the 2026 ceremony, Diane Warren had earned 17 Best Original Song nominations and lost every time, the most career Oscar nominations by any person without a competitive win. She received an honorary Oscar in 2022, but that does not count as a competitive trophy.
questions.why: The slicer points at the single most-nominated never-winner across the whole Academy, which feels like it should be an actor or director, not a behind-the-scenes songwriter.
questions.more: Sound mixer Greg P. Russell had previously held the mark with 16 nominations and no win.
questions.source: Variety, 'Diane Warren Vows I'll Be Back at Oscars... Most Nominated Person to Not Win a Competitive Academy Award' (2026); Britannica, 'Who Has Been Nominated for the Most Oscars but Never Won?'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · most acting Osc
questions.dimension: most acting Oscar nominations, no win
questions.q: Two performers share the record for the most acting Oscar nominations without ever winning, each with eight. Name either one.
questions.detail: Peter O'Toole went 0-for-8 in the Best Actor category over four decades, and Glenn Close has gone 0-for-8 across lead and supporting roles. They are tied for the most acting nominations with no competitive win.
questions.why: Both names sit in the famous-actor set, but the player has to recall that neither ever actually won, and that they are tied at the very top.
questions.more: O'Toole accepted an honorary Oscar in 2003.
questions.source: Britannica, 'Who Has Been Nominated for the Most Oscars but Never Won?'; Parade, 'These Hollywood Icons Have the Most Oscar Nominations With No Wins for Acting'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · Best Director n
questions.dimension: Best Director nominations, no win (a major name)
questions.q: Among directors who never won the Best Director Oscar, which one was nominated five times, including for 'Rear Window' and 'Psycho,' yet went home empty-handed every time?
questions.detail: Hitchcock earned five Best Director nominations: Rebecca, Lifeboat, Spellbound, Rear Window, and Psycho. He never won the directing award, despite Rebecca taking Best Picture.
questions.why: The film clues narrow it to a short list of suspense masters; the surprise is that arguably the most famous director of all never won for directing.
questions.more: He later received the Academy's Irving G. Thalberg Memorial Award.
questions.source: ScreenRant, 'Why Alfred Hitchcock Never Won An Oscar'; SlashFilm, 'When Should Alfred Hitchcock Have Won His Oscar?'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · most Primetime
questions.dimension: most Primetime Emmy acting nominations, no win
questions.q: Which performer was nominated for a Primetime Emmy 18 times without ever winning, the most acting Emmy nominations any never-winner has piled up?
questions.detail: Lansbury collected 18 Primetime Emmy nominations and never won, 12 of them for playing Jessica Fletcher on 'Murder, She Wrote.'
questions.why: The TV-legend set is large, but the player must land on the one with the longest losing streak rather than a more recent star.
questions.more: She did win Tony, Grammy, and honorary Oscar recognition over her career.
questions.source: Wikipedia, 'List of awards and nominations received by Angela Lansbury'; Looper, 'Angela Lansbury Was Nominated For 18 Emmy Awards But Never Won'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · most Grammy nom
questions.dimension: most Grammy nominations by a performer, no win
questions.q: As of 2025, which rapper and singer became the most-nominated performing artist in Grammy history never to win, sitting at 18 nominations and no wins?
questions.detail: Post Malone reached 18 Grammy nominations with no wins, passing the previous mark and becoming the most-nominated performing artist never to take one home.
questions.why: The player has to filter to a current chart-topping star whose lack of a Grammy is genuinely surprising given his sales.
questions.more: Bjork and Snoop Dogg trail him among performers with 16 nominations each and no wins.
questions.source: Consequence, 'Post Malone is now the most-nominated artist without a Grammy win' (2025); Taste of Country, 'Post Malone Just Set a Dubious Grammy Awards Record'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · most Hot 100 To
questions.dimension: most Hot 100 Top 40 hits, no No. 1
questions.q: Which group landed 20 Top 40 hits on the Billboard Hot 100, more than any band in chart history, yet never once reached No. 1?
questions.detail: ELO charted 20 Top 40 hits on the Hot 100 without ever topping it; their highest peak was 'Don't Bring Me Down' at No. 4 in 1979.
questions.why: The set of hit-making bands is wide, but the player must recall a string of familiar songs that all stalled short of the summit.
questions.more: Other ELO peaks include 'Telephone Line' (No. 7) and 'Shine a Little Love' (No. 8).
questions.source: Top40Weekly, 'Electric Light Orchestra Biography'; Media Fandom, 'Electric Light Orchestra' chart records

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · most weeks at N
questions.dimension: most weeks at No. 2, never No. 1 (single track)
questions.q: Two songs are tied for the most weeks stuck at No. 2 on the Billboard Hot 100 without ever reaching No. 1, each spending 10 weeks there. Name either song or its artist.
questions.answer_subject: 'Waiting for a Girl Like You' (Foreigner) or 'Work It' (Missy Elliott)
questions.state: 'Waiting for a Girl Like You' (Foreigner) or 'Work It' (Missy Elliott)
questions.detail: Foreigner's 'Waiting for a Girl Like You' and Missy Elliott's 'Work It' each logged 10 weeks at No. 2 on the Hot 100 and never hit the top.
questions.why: The slicer measures a single song's time stranded at runner-up, a different axis from career totals, and the answer pairs two very different eras.
questions.more: Both were blocked from the top spot by other long-running No. 1 hits of their day.
questions.source: Billboard, 'Biggest No. 2 Hits Ever: Songs That Missed No. 1 on Hot 100'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · most CMA Award
questions.dimension: most CMA Award nominations, no win
questions.q: In country music, which act has racked up 29 CMA Award nominations across its career, the most of anyone, without ever winning a single one?
questions.detail: The Bellamy Brothers hold the all-time CMA record with 29 nominations and zero wins.
questions.why: Players know the country-act set, but the winless king is a long-running duo, not a flashier modern star.
questions.more: Chris Stapleton holds the never-won mark within the Entertainer of the Year category alone.
questions.source: Taste of Country and CMA records via '19 Country Stars With the Most Grammy Nominations and Zero Wins'; Wikipedia CMA category records

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · most Oscar nomi
questions.dimension: most Oscar nominations by a film, zero wins
questions.q: Among films, which two share the record for the most Academy Award nominations without a single win, each nominated 11 times and shut out completely?
questions.detail: Herbert Ross's 'The Turning Point' (1977) and Steven Spielberg's 'The Color Purple' (1985) each drew 11 nominations and won nothing.
questions.why: The famous-films set is large, and the surprise is that a celebrated Spielberg picture is tied for the most futile Oscar night ever.
questions.more: Both were also nominated for Best Picture.
questions.source: Guinness World Records, 'Most Oscar nominations without winning - film'; SlashFilm on 'The Color Purple' record

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · most PGA Tour w
questions.dimension: most PGA Tour wins, no major
questions.q: Which golfer won 29 PGA Tour events, more than anyone else in tour history, yet never captured a major championship?
questions.detail: Cooper won 29 PGA Tour titles without a major, the most of any golfer, finishing runner-up in majors four times.
questions.why: The slicer routes away from modern near-misses toward an early-era leader who genuinely tops the list.
questions.more: Doug Sanders, with 20 wins and a famous missed putt at the 1970 Open, is among the next on the list.
questions.source: Golf Compendium, 'Most PGA Tour Victories Without Winning a Major'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · most Tony actin
questions.dimension: most Tony acting nominations, no win
questions.q: Which performer holds the record for the most Tony Award nominations without ever winning, going 0-for-6 in shows like 'Cabaret' and 'Fiddler on the Roof'?
questions.detail: Burstein earned six Tony nominations across leading Broadway roles without a win, the most by any performer at the time.
questions.why: The Broadway-star set is right-sized, and the player must recall that this acclaimed character actor still has no Tony.
questions.more: Costume designer Jane Greenwood holds a separate non-performer mark with a long streak of nominations before her first win.
questions.source: The Broadway Producers, '5 Performers with the Most Tony Nominations without a Win'; Wikipedia, 'List of Tony Award records'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · most Nobel Priz
questions.dimension: most Nobel Prize nominations, no win
questions.q: Which physicist was nominated for the Nobel Prize a record 84 times, more than anyone in history, and never won it, despite mentoring several future laureates?
questions.detail: Sommerfeld received 84 Nobel nominations, the most ever, without winning, while his students Heisenberg, Pauli, Debye, and Bethe all became laureates.
questions.why: Among famous early-quantum physicists, the player must land on the teacher who out-nominated everyone yet was passed over.
questions.more: Nobel nomination archives are released after 50 years, which is how the count is known.
questions.source: The Vintage News, 'The Man with the Most Nobel Nominations - And no Wins'; OpenMind, 'Sommerfeld: the Eternal Nobel Candidate'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · most Best Direc
questions.dimension: most Best Director nominations among never-winners
questions.q: Which director was nominated six times for the Best Director Oscar, the most of anyone who never won that award, for films including 'National Velvet' and 'The Yearling'?
questions.detail: Brown earned six Best Director nominations and never won, the most for any director who never took the award. Robert Altman, who also never won competitively, had seven directing nominations.
questions.why: This is a deeper cut than Hitchcock, requiring the player to reason past the famous near-misses to the actual record-holder.
questions.more: Altman received an honorary Oscar; Brown did not.
questions.source: Far Out, 'Which director has most Oscar nominations without winning?'; Oscar records on Clarence Brown's six directing nominations

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · most Eurovision
questions.dimension: most Eurovision second-place finishes
questions.q: Which country has finished as the Eurovision Song Contest runner-up a record 16 times without ever, in modern counting, holding the most second places of any nation?
questions.detail: The UK has placed second 16 times at Eurovision, the most runner-up finishes of any country, even though it has also won five times.
questions.why: The slicer measures runner-up frequency, not entries or wins, so the player must distinguish 'most silver medals' from overall success.
questions.more: Cyprus separately holds the record for most contests entered without a win.
questions.source: Brilliant Maps, Eurovision second-place data; Eurovision Song Contest Wiki, 'Overall Records'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · most consecutiv
questions.dimension: most consecutive Daytime Emmy losses before a first win
questions.q: Which soap-opera star became famous for losing the Daytime Emmy 18 times in a row before finally winning in 1999, the longest such losing streak before a win?
questions.detail: Lucci lost the Daytime Emmy for lead actress 18 straight times as Erica Kane on 'All My Children' before winning in 1999.
questions.why: The slicer is about the streak itself, and the answer is the performer whose name became shorthand for being a perennial runner-up.
questions.more: Presenter Shemar Moore opened the envelope by shouting 'The streak is over.'
questions.source: CBS News, 'An Emmy, Finally'; AOL, 'Susan Lucci recalls stunned disbelief when she won first Daytime Emmy after losing 18 times'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · most Top 40 alb
questions.dimension: most Top 40 albums, no Hot 100 No. 1 single
questions.q: Which band recently set the record for the most Top 40 albums in Billboard 200 history, passing Elvis Presley and Frank Sinatra, yet never once landed a No. 1 single on the Hot 100?
questions.detail: The Grateful Dead broke the record for most Top 40 albums on the Billboard 200, surpassing Elvis and Sinatra, but their highest-charting single, 'Touch of Grey,' peaked at only No. 6 on the Hot 100.
questions.why: The slicer pits massive album and touring success against a complete absence of hit singles, pushing the player toward a jam band rather than a pop act.
questions.more: The band placed only a handful of singles on the Hot 100 across its whole career.
questions.source: Billboard, 'Grateful Dead Breaks Record for Most Top 40 Albums on Billboard 200'; Billboard chart-beat coverage of 'Touch of Grey'

questions.cat: Always a bridesmaid (best-without records) · honorary Oscar,
questions.dimension: honorary Oscar, only one competitive nod and no win (high-grossing star)
questions.q: Which actor and singer received an honorary Academy Award in 2022 yet, despite being one of the most prolific and bankable stars in Hollywood, has earned just one competitive Oscar nomination and never won?
questions.detail: Jackson was given an honorary Oscar in 2022. His only competitive nomination came for 'Pulp Fiction' in 1995, and he has never won a competitive Academy Award.
questions.why: Among ubiquitous, hugely successful actors, the player must recall that this one has a near-empty competitive Oscar record.
questions.more: He has openly said he believes he should have won for 'Pulp Fiction.'
questions.source: GoldDerby, '2022 Governors Awards profile: Samuel L. Jackson to receive Honorary Oscar'; Variety, 'Samuel L. Jackson Says I Should've Won an Oscar by Now'
